# The Foundation > Open Collective Europe – Legal Information > Partnerships > Platform and ecosystem partnerships
**Open Finance Consortium (OFICO)** \
  We work closely with the team behind the Open Collective platform, which powers our financial and collaborative infrastructure. This partnership ensures that OCE’s services are seamlessly integrated with the platform’s tools and features.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: The Foundation > Open Collective Europe – Legal Information > Partnerships > Platform and ecosystem partnerships

content:
# The Foundation > Open Collective Europe – Legal Information > Partnerships > Platform and ecosystem partnerships
**Open Source Collective (OSC)**\
  A US-based nonprofit fiscal host focused on open source communities. We share platform practices and collaborate on shared needs across the hosting ecosystem, especially for tech-focused initiatives.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: The Foundation > Open Collective Europe – Legal Information > Partnerships > Platform and ecosystem partnerships

content:
# The Foundation > Open Collective Europe – Legal Information > Partnerships > Financial and compliance infrastructure
**King Baudouin Foundation (KBF)**\
  Our partnership with KBF enables recognized charitable status across Europe and the US. This makes it possible for donors — including individuals and institutional funders — to benefit from tax-deductibility when contributing to eligible initiatives.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: The Foundation > Open Collective Europe – Legal Information > Partnerships > Financial and compliance infrastructure

content:

  Open Collective Europe collaborates with Myriad USA to facilitate **tax-deductible donations from U.S.-based donors**.

Manage the legal and operational "architecture" of new complex projects.

content:
# Grant & Operations Lead (Laura) > 1. Core Mission
**Onboarding & Mandate Authority:**
Ensure all "Mandate Agreements" for strategic initiatives are bulletproof and aligned with the Foundation's public interest mission.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Grant & Operations Lead (Laura) > 1. Core Mission

content:
# Grant & Operations Lead (Laura) > Boundaries & Escalation (Strict)
**Supervision of Back-Office Flow:** While Ivan executes the payments, Laura defines the operational standards and priorities he must follow. She ensures the "Back-Office" scales at the same pace as the "Front-Office."

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Grant & Operations Lead (Laura) > Boundaries & Escalation (Strict)

content:
# Grant & Operations Lead (Laura) > Boundaries & Escalation (Strict)
**Strict Fiduciary Alignment:** As a "Sparring Partner," she must challenge any project or expenditure that risks the Foundation’s "Public Utility" status or deviates from the "Indirect Representation" model.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Grant & Operations Lead (Laura) > Boundaries & Escalation (Strict)
Grant & Operations Lead (Laura) > Boundaries & Escalation (Strict)

content: # Creation and Sending of Mandate Agreements > Responsibilities 2. Template selection\ \ Agreement templates are prepared by the legal team (external lawyers) and stored on Google Drive - [OCEF](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1Zs26YrAQ5vav4btWBCuLLb2RMcK3orAE?usp=drive_link) and [OSE](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1_3wi8ooSbW4tlwynkOIWdZ8QZnDGYoG8?usp=drive_link).\ Operations must use the template indicated by the Director.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Creation and Sending of Mandate Agreements > Responsibilities

content: # Creation and Sending of Mandate Agreements > Responsibilities 3. Drafting the agreement\ \ Operations completes the selected template with the relevant information. At least two administrators of the initiative must be included, with their legal names, addresses, email addresses, and, where required, ID numbers.

content:
# Receiving Funds from the U.S. > 1. Tax-deductible donations from individual donors > Step-by-step process:
1. **Collective request**\
   The collective contacts OCE to request support for receiving U.S. tax-deductible donations.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Receiving Funds from the U.S. > 1. Tax-deductible donations from individual donors > Step-by-step process:

content:
# Receiving Funds from the U.S. > 1. Tax-deductible donations from individual donors > Step-by-step process:
2. **Setup via Every.org**\
   We provide the collective with a link and instructions to set up a fundraising page on [Every.org](https://www.every.org/), a U.S.-based donation platform.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Receiving Funds from the U.S. > 1. Tax-deductible donations from individual donors > Step-by-step process:

content:
# Receiving Funds from the U.S. > 1. Tax-deductible donations from individual donors > Step-by-step process:
3. **Donations via Every.org**\
   U.S. donors contribute through Every.org and receive a tax-deductible receipt.\
   The collective does not interact with Myriad USA (Every.org's fiscal sponsor).

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Receiving Funds from the U.S. > 1. Tax-deductible donations from individual donors > Step-by-step process:

content:
# Receiving Funds from the U.S. > 1. Tax-deductible donations from individual donors > Step-by-step process:
4. **End of campaign**\
   When the collective informs us that they are done fundraising via Every.org, we initiate the next step.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Receiving Funds from the U.S. > 1. Tax-deductible donations from individual donors > Step-by-step process:

content:
# Receiving Funds from the U.S. > 1. Tax-deductible donations from individual donors > Step-by-step process:
5. **Fund transfer from Myriad USA**\
   OCE contacts Myriad USA (the fiscal sponsor holding the funds) and requests the transfer of the collected donations. This is treated as a grant from Myriad USA to the Foundation.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Receiving Funds from the U.S. > 1. Tax-deductible donations from individual donors > Step-by-step process:

content:
# Receiving Funds from the U.S. > 1. Tax-deductible donations from individual donors > Step-by-step process:
6. **Funds are received and credited**\
   Once OCE receives the funds from Myriad USA, we allocate them to the relevant collective on the Open Collective platform.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Receiving Funds from the U.S. > 1. Tax-deductible donations from individual donors > Step-by-step process:

content:
# Receiving Funds from the U.S. > 1. Tax-deductible donations from individual donors > Fees:
**OCE applies its standard 10% hosting fee** on all funds received.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Receiving Funds from the U.S. > 1. Tax-deductible donations from individual donors > Fees:

content:
# Receiving Funds from the U.S. > 1. Tax-deductible donations from individual donors > Fees:
**Myriad USA also deducts a fee** (typically between 1–2%) before sending the grant to OCE.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Receiving Funds from the U.S. > 1. Tax-deductible donations from individual donors > Fees:

content:
# Receiving Funds from the U.S. > 1. Tax-deductible donations from individual donors > Fees:
Collectives should be informed that **only the OCE fee (10%) applies to their received amount**, regardless of Myriad's internal handling fee.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Receiving Funds from the U.S. > 1. Tax-deductible donations from individual donors > Fees:

content:
# Receiving Funds from the U.S. > 2. Grants from U.S.-based private foundations > Step-by-step process:
1. **Service agreement**\
   Before any grant can be accepted, a **service agreement** must be signed between OCE and the collective.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Receiving Funds from the U.S. > 2. Grants from U.S.-based private foundations > Step-by-step process:

content:
# Receiving Funds from the U.S. > 2. Grants from U.S.-based private foundations > Step-by-step process:
2. **Grant approval**\
   The collective leads the grant application process with the U.S. foundation.\
   Once approved, OCE steps in as the legal recipient.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Receiving Funds from the U.S. > 2. Grants from U.S.-based private foundations > Step-by-step process:

content:
# Receiving Funds from the U.S. > 2. Grants from U.S.-based private foundations > Step-by-step process:
3. **Direct funder coordination**\
   OCE signs the **grant agreement**, provides its **banking information**, and shares the **Equivalency Determination Certificate** with the funder.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Receiving Funds from the U.S. > 2. Grants from U.S.-based private foundations > Step-by-step process:

content:
# Receiving Funds from the U.S. > 2. Grants from U.S.-based private foundations > Step-by-step process:
4. **Fund reception and credit**\
   Upon receipt of the grant, OCE allocates the amount to the collective’s balance on the platform.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Receiving Funds from the U.S. > 2. Grants from U.S.-based private foundations > Step-by-step process:

content:
# Receiving Funds from the U.S. > 2. Grants from U.S.-based private foundations > Fees:
OCE applies its **standard 10% fee** on all grants received from U.S.-based foundations.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Receiving Funds from the U.S. > 2. Grants from U.S.-based private foundations > Fees:

content:
# Receiving Funds from the U.S. > 2. Grants from U.S.-based private foundations > Fees:
No additional external fee (e.g., Myriad) applies in this scenario.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Receiving Funds from the U.S. > 2. Grants from U.S.-based private foundations > Fees:

# Receiving Funds from Europe > General principles
Open Collective Europe Foundation is legally based in Belgium and recognized as a **public utility foundation** (*fondation d’utilité publique*).

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Receiving Funds from Europe > General principles

content:
# Receiving Funds from Europe > General principles
This status is generally well received by EU-based foundations, but each institution has its own due diligence and contractual requirements.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Receiving Funds from Europe > General principles

content:
# Receiving Funds from Europe > General principles
We typically position ourselves as the **fiscal host and legal recipient** of funds, and sign agreements directly with funders.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Receiving Funds from Europe > General principles

content:
# Receiving Funds from Europe > Process (flexible case-by-case)
1. **The collective leads the conversation with the foundation**\
   In most cases, the initiative opens the discussion or applies for funding.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Receiving Funds from Europe > Process (flexible case-by-case)

content:
# Receiving Funds from Europe > Process (flexible case-by-case)
2. **OCE is looped in once the foundation requires a legal recipient**\
   At that point, we review the documentation and coordinate directly with the foundation’s grants or legal team.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Receiving Funds from Europe > Process (flexible case-by-case)

content:
# Receiving Funds from Europe > Process (flexible case-by-case)
3. **Agreement and reception**\
   OCE signs the grant agreement and receives the funds, which are then credited to the relevant collective.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Receiving Funds from Europe > Process (flexible case-by-case)

content:
# Receiving Funds from Europe > Process (flexible case-by-case)
4. **Documentation**\
   As always, the service agreement with the collective must be in place before receiving any funds.\
   All related documents must be stored in the Drive folder.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Receiving Funds from Europe > Process (flexible case-by-case)

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Mondays
**Bank reconciliation and estimate — first round of the week**

  Match incoming bank transfers with the Open Collective platform. Read the Bank transfers for the Collectives chapter for details.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Mondays

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Mondays
During the reconciliation:

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Mondays

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Mondays
During the reconciliation:
Identify unknown sources.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Mondays

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Mondays
During the reconciliation:
Tag transfers requiring follow-up.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Mondays

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Mondays
During the reconciliation:
Assign unidentified contributions to the holding fund, following the procedure for unclaimed or unreconciliable funds.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Mondays

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Mondays
During the reconciliation:
Estimate available funds and expected payout capacity for the week or upcoming period.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Mondays

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Mondays
**Top up Wise accounts**\
  Check Wise balances and request funds from KBC via Jean-François when necessary.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Mondays

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Mondays
**Check blocked or delayed expenses**

  Review expenses that are blocked, delayed, incomplete, or awaiting clarification.

  Follow up with collective admins or submitters where needed.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Mondays

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Mondays
**Check PayPal and Stripe disputes**

  Review whether there are any open disputes on PayPal or Stripe. Handle disputes according to the relevant dispute guidelines: confirm the transaction, gather evidence where appropriate, coordinate with OFiCo or OC Inc. if needed, and issue a refund only when required or approved.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Mondays

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Mondays
**Review incoming grant-related communications**

  Check for incoming grant-related emails, funder communications, due diligence requests, or banking documentation. Sync with JF and the grants team where needed.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Mondays

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Mondays
**Review Freshdesk tickets related to financial operations**

  Review and address Freshdesk tickets related to payments, expenses, bank transfers, refunds, grant payments, invoices, or other financial operations.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Mondays

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Mondays
**New collective onboarding — finance side**

  Handle finance-related onboarding steps for new collectives, including setup checks, payment configuration, banking information, and any related financial documentation.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Mondays

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Mondays
**Review incoming grant-related comms**\
  Check oce@ inbox for new grant agreements, funder updates, or due diligence requests.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Mondays

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Tuesdays
**Expense payouts**\
  Process approved and ready-to-pay expenses through Wise, PayPal, or bank transfer.

  The payout order is:

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Tuesdays

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Tuesdays
**Expense payouts**\
1. OCEF USD

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Tuesdays

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Tuesdays
**Expense payouts**\
2. OCEF EUR

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Tuesdays

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Tuesdays
**Expense payouts**\
3. OSE

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Tuesdays

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Wednesdays
**Application review and collective exchanges**\
  Evaluate new applications and exchange with collectives about missing information, eligibility, onboarding steps, or financial setup.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Wednesdays

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Wednesdays
**Collective onboarding** \
  Support the onboarding of new collectives, especially where financial setup, missing information, or platform configuration needs to be clarified.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Wednesdays

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Wednesdays
**Freshdesk review** \
  Review Freshdesk tickets and respond to finance-related questions or route them to the appropriate team member when needed.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Wednesdays

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Thursdays
**Expense payout day** \
  Process approved and ready-to-pay expenses through Wise, PayPal, or bank transfer. The payout order is:

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Thursdays

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Thursdays
1. OCEF USD

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Thursdays

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Thursdays
2. OCEF EUR

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Thursdays

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Thursdays
3. OSE\
   After payouts are processed, perform a second control round to check whether submitters have responded to incomplete expense requests.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Thursdays

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Thursdays
**Check incoming grants on bank accounts**\
  Review bank accounts for incoming grants and ensure that they are identified, documented, and prepared for correct allocation on the platform.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Thursdays

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Thursdays
**Process operational expenses** \
  Process OCE operational expenses as needed. OCE Operational expenses are mostly paid manually via KBC Budget EUR account. Platform fees to OFiTech can be paid via Wise transfer to OFiTech Wise account. \
  Pay particular attention to **Wise virtual card expenses**, as these must all be added manually to the platform.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Thursdays

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Thursdays
**Collective onboarding** \
  Support onboarding calls and follow-up with collectives, especially where financial setup, documentation, or payout readiness needs to be confirmed.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Thursdays

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Fridays
**Review urgent Freshdesk tickets and late payouts** \
  Review urgent finance-related Freshdesk tickets, unresolved payout issues, and any late payments that need attention before the weekend.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Fridays

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Fridays
**Reconciliation of transactions in Zoho/Odoo** \
  Reconcile transactions in Zoho/Odoo and verify that all bank and Wise transactions are reflected correctly on the Open Collective platform. \
  This includes checking that incoming transfers, outgoing payouts, operational expenses, and manual adjustments are properly mirrored across systems.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Weekly Tasks > Fridays

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Monthly Tasks
**Platform–bank reconciliation summary**\
  Double-check totals between the Open Collective platform and actual accounts.\
  Note discrepancies for escalation.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Monthly Tasks

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Monthly Tasks
**Fund allocation follow-up**\
  Remind collectives about updating their budget if large grants or restricted-use funds were received.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Monthly Tasks

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Monthly Tasks
**GitHub Sponsors income update**

  Update GitHub Sponsors income together with Open Collective Support / OFiCo Operations, currently coordinated with Sudharaka. Ensure that the GitHub Sponsors report is received, shared, uploaded, and checked for accuracy. Currently only on OSE.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Monthly Tasks

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Monthly Tasks
**Invoice and corporate donation requests**\
  Respond to companies requesting invoices or fiscal receipts for large gifts.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Monthly Tasks

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Monthly Tasks
**Internal budget review**\
  Track incoming fees, grants, and expenses for OCE's own operations.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Monthly Tasks

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Quarterly Tasks
**Accounting and VAT closing**

  Coordinate with the accountants to close the quarter for VAT and verify their accounting records. Schedule calls with the accountants when needed to clarify discrepancies, missing documentation, or treatment of specific transactions.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Quarterly Tasks

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Quarterly Tasks
**Grant tracking review**\
  Update the list of active grants, deadlines, and reporting obligations.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Quarterly Tasks

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Quarterly Tasks
**Funder report prep**\
  Collaborate with the admin collective (or relevant lead) to generate financial reports as needed by funders.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Quarterly Tasks

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > Quarterly Tasks
**Review crypto transactions and Every.org flows**\
  Ensure that all related donations are properly documented and reconciled.

  For ongoing Every.org fundraisers, check whether a payout should be requested and coordinate the follow-up when funds need to be transferred.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > Quarterly Tasks

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > As-Needed Tasks
**New collective onboarding (finance side)**\
  Set up Drive folder, check for service agreement, verify Stripe/PayPal/Wise linkage.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > As-Needed Tasks

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > As-Needed Tasks
**Large grant setup**\
  Ensure banking details and documentation are provided to the funder.\
  Confirm allocation of funds on the platform.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > As-Needed Tasks

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > As-Needed Tasks
**Refunds or payment disputes**\
  Issue refunds as requested on the platform, under **Transactions**, after verifying that the refund is required or approved.

  Coordinate with OFiCo when necessary.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > As-Needed Tasks

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > As-Needed Tasks
**Bank admin changes**\
  Manage access rights or initiate new banking connections (e.g., Wise admin handover).

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Financial Ops Task Overview > As-Needed Tasks

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > As-Needed Tasks
**Document exceptions and edge cases**

  Document exceptions or unusual cases in the appropriate place, depending on the situation:

content:

**Document exceptions and edge cases**
**GitBook**, if the case creates a reusable process or precedent

content:

**Document exceptions and edge cases**
**Drive**, within the relevant collective’s folder, if it concerns a specific collective

content:

**Document exceptions and edge cases**
**Slack**, if the case requires short-term coordination or visibility

content:
# Financial Ops Task Overview > As-Needed Tasks
**Flag blockers early**

  Flag blockers as early as possible to avoid delays, payment errors, reconciliation issues, or unclear responsibilities.

content:

content:
# Bank Transfers for the Collectives > General principles
Contributions made through Stripe and PayPal are automated. Manual wire transfers received directly into OCE bank accounts must be matched with the correct beneficiaries. Currently, this matching is done through **Zoho Books**, but the goal is to transfer this process entirely to the platform’s **Ledger → Bank Sync** tools. On OCEF, manual contributions mainly arrive in two bank accounts:

# Bank Transfers for the Collectives > Weekly procedure
**IMPORTANT:** For grants added to collectives, do not apply the host fees - calculate the overhead ahead in base on the defined percentage with the collective, upload to the collective's balance the net amount, and add the bank charges. Overhead should be added as a separate contribution directly to OCE operations balance. \
  The tech team is working on finding a solution for this operation, similar to the host fees function.

content:
# Project management > 1. Onboarding & Collective Growth
**Vetting & Tiering:** Reviewing new applications based on the **Project Tier Matrix** to assess risk and alignment with the Foundation’s mission.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Project management > 1. Onboarding & Collective Growth

content:
# Project management > 1. Onboarding & Collective Growth
**Collective Onboarding**: Guiding new initiatives through the administrative and legal setup, ensuring all collectives agree to our General Terms and Conditions. This ensures that administrators are aware of their legal responsibilities, particularly regarding financial transparency and the requirement to provide valid fiscal documentation for every expense.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Project management > 1. Onboarding & Collective Growth

content:
# Project management > 1. Onboarding & Collective Growth
**Growth Strategy:** Proposing and implementing communication initiatives to attract high-impact collectives and mission-aligned donors.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Project management > 1. Onboarding & Collective Growth

content:
# Project management > 2. Community Support & Communication
**Freshdesk & Email Management:** Handling specialized and complex inquiries. Purely administrative tasks (standard KYC/invoice status) are delegated to the Operations Assistant, while project-specific support remains under Project Management.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Project management > 2. Community Support & Communication

content:
# Project management > 2. Community Support & Communication
**Discord Engagement:** Active presence on community channels to provide real-time guidance and foster ecosystem transparency.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Project management > 2. Community Support & Communication

content:
# Project management > 2. Community Support & Communication
**Stakeholder Education:** Continuous pedagogical support to help members navigate the "Indirect Representation" model.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Project management > 2. Community Support & Communication

content:
# Project management > 3. Fund Management: Global Statelessness Fund (GSF)
**Grant Lifecycle Management:** Regular updates of grant agreements and ensuring legal compliance for all re-granting activities.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Project management > 3. Fund Management: Global Statelessness Fund (GSF)

content:
# Project management > 4. Documentation & Tools
**Guideline Maintenance:** Keeping the official documentation up to date with the latest legal requirements and platform features.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Project management > 4. Documentation & Tools

content:
# Project management > 4. Documentation & Tools
**Reporting & Visuals:** Creation of strategic supports, including presentations (PPT) and internal memos for governance meetings.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Project management > 4. Documentation & Tools

content:
# Project management > 4. Documentation & Tools
**Process Improvement:** Identifying bottlenecks in the hosting flow and proposing technical or procedural solutions.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Project management > 4. Documentation & Tools
Project management > 4. Documentation & Tools

content:
# Project Tier Matrix > 🔴 TIER 3 – Strategic & Internal Funds > 🛠️ Operational Rules for the Team:
1. Incoming Projects: All new applications are filtered by Ivan. If a project meets Tier 2 or Tier 3 criteria, it must be automatically transferred to Laura for architectural setup.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Project Tier Matrix > 🔴 TIER 3 – Strategic & Internal Funds > 🛠️ Operational Rules for the Team:

content:
# Project Tier Matrix > 🔴 TIER 3 – Strategic & Internal Funds > 🛠️ Operational Rules for the Team:
2. The "Veto" Rule: For all Tiers, Ivan retains a compliance veto. Even if Laura or the Director approves a project, Ivan must block a payment if the invoice or receipt is legally non-compliant.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Project Tier Matrix > 🔴 TIER 3 – Strategic & Internal Funds > 🛠️ Operational Rules for the Team:

content:
# Project Tier Matrix > 🔴 TIER 3 – Strategic & Internal Funds > 🛠️ Operational Rules for the Team:
3. Communication Flow: \* Tier 1: Ivan communicates directly with the Collective.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Project Tier Matrix > 🔴 TIER 3 – Strategic & Internal Funds > 🛠️ Operational Rules for the Team:

content:
# Project Tier Matrix > 🔴 TIER 3 – Strategic & Internal Funds > 🛠️ Operational Rules for the Team:
3. Communication Flow: \* Tier 1: Ivan communicates directly with the Collective.
Tier 2 & 3: Laura handles the relationship; Ivan handles the backend and accounting liaison.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Project Tier Matrix > 🔴 TIER 3 – Strategic & Internal Funds > 🛠️ Operational Rules for the Team:

# About Open Source Europe > Service Overview
**Fiscal hosting for open source projects:** We provide a legal umbrella for developers, maintainers, and communities, allowing them to operate without creating their own legal entity.

# About Open Source Europe > OSE vs Open Collective
1. **Open Source Europe:** An independent, Belgian-based non-profit association (ASBL/vzw) that provides fiscal hosting and fund management services.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: About Open Source Europe > OSE vs Open Collective

content:
# About Open Source Europe > OSE vs Open Collective
2. **Open Collective:** The open-source software platform we use to deliver these services. The technology is developed and maintained by the **Open Finance Consortium**, a collective effort dedicated to building transparent financial infrastructure for the commons.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: About Open Source Europe > OSE vs Open Collective

content:

**Simplified Legal Framework:** Skip the complexity of non-profit incorporation, bank account openings, and statutory bookkeeping.

**Professional Back-office:** We handle the administrative heavy lifting, including invoicing, VAT compliance, and annual financial reporting.

content:

**Radical Transparency:** Every transaction is logged in real-time, building immediate trust with your backers and contributors.

1. **Mission Alignment:** Projects must actively support the development or sustainability of open source software.

content:

# Terms of Service > Summary: What you agree to by joining OCE
**You stay in control**: OCE manages a fund on your behalf, but the money belongs to your initiative. OCE is not the owner of the contributions.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Terms of Service > Summary: What you agree to by joining OCE

content:
# Terms of Service > Summary: What you agree to by joining OCE
**You are legally and fiscally independent**: Your initiative is not a legal extension of OCE. You are responsible for your own activities, including local tax and legal compliance.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Terms of Service > Summary: What you agree to by joining OCE

content:
# Terms of Service > Summary: What you agree to by joining OCE
**Transparency is mandatory**: All income and expenses must be tracked publicly through your Open Collective page.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Terms of Service > Summary: What you agree to by joining OCE

content:
# Terms of Service > Summary: What you agree to by joining OCE
**Funds must serve a charitable mission**: Contributions can only be used to support your initiative’s stated purpose, in line with OCE’s values and Belgian nonprofit law.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Terms of Service > Summary: What you agree to by joining OCE

content:
# Terms of Service > Summary: What you agree to by joining OCE
**Termination is possible**: Either party can end the agreement with 30 days' notice. Remaining funds must be redirected to a charitable purpose.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Terms of Service > Summary: What you agree to by joining OCE

**Alignment with OCE's mission and impact areas**: Your activities should be charitable and aimed at benefiting the public.

**Minimum of two administrators**: Your collective must have at least two admins with real names.

content:

**No separate legal entity**: Your initiative should not have its own legal existence/entity separate from OCE.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: How to apply > Before You Apply

content:

**Global scope**: Your initiative can be based anywhere in the world, not just in Europe.

# Eligibility > ✅ Eligibility Criteria
**Align with OCE's mission and impact areas**: Your activities should be charitable and aimed at benefiting the public.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Eligibility > ✅ Eligibility Criteria

content:
# Eligibility > ✅ Eligibility Criteria
**Be non-profit in nature**: While you can charge membership fees or organize paid activities, profits should not be distributed to members.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Eligibility > ✅ Eligibility Criteria

content:

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Eligibility > ✅ Eligibility Criteria

**Have at least two administrators**: Your collective must have a minimum of two team members with real names to manage the collective.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Eligibility > ✅ Eligibility Criteria

content:

**Maintain appropriate content standards**: Content promoting violence, hate speech, pornography, or any other material deemed inappropriate or offensive is not permitted.

# Eligibility > 📄 Important Considerations
**Fund Management**: Funds raised under OCE's umbrella become part of the foundation's structure. If you decide to leave OCE or close your account, remaining funds must be disbursed to the community or transferred to other associations.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Eligibility > 📄 Important Considerations

content:
# Eligibility > 📄 Important Considerations
**Compliance**: OCE assesses potential compliance risks associated with initiatives, including alignment with legal frameworks and avoidance of activities that could be linked to terrorism or extremism financing.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Eligibility > 📄 Important Considerations

# Application Questions > Required Information
**Location:** Specify the city and country where your initiative is primarily based. While OCE hosts initiatives globally, understanding your location helps us provide better support.

**Administrators:** Ensure your collective has at least two administrators with real names. Administrators have full permissions to manage the collective’s settings and finances.

# Basics > ✅ Top 5 Things to Remember
1. **Contracts and Agreements**: Contact us at <oce@opencollective.com> if you need to sign any contracts or agreements, or have questions about services or policies.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Basics > ✅ Top 5 Things to Remember

content:
# Basics > ✅ Top 5 Things to Remember
2. **Official Documentation**: For all necessary paperwork that may be required in grant applications, refer to our [Official Info and Docs](https://docs.opencollective.com/oceurope/official-info-and-docs).

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Basics > ✅ Top 5 Things to Remember

content:
# Basics > ✅ Top 5 Things to Remember
3. **Community Updates**: Use the **Updates** feature to post general, mid-year, and annual updates or action alerts to your donor community. We require at least one update per year.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Basics > ✅ Top 5 Things to Remember

content:
# Basics > ✅ Top 5 Things to Remember
4. **Submitting Expenses**: FAQs about how to submit expenses (i.e., get paid or spend money) are available [here](https://docs.opencollective.com/help/expenses-and-getting-paid/submitting-expenses).

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Basics > ✅ Top 5 Things to Remember

content:
# Basics > ✅ Top 5 Things to Remember
5. **Donations Only – Not-for-Profit Model:**\
   As an unincorporated initiative on OCE, you're set up to receive **donations and grants** in support of your work. Just a heads-up: it's not possible to sell products or services or operate commercially through the platform — we're here to help you thrive as a community-driven, non-profit project!

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Basics > ✅ Top 5 Things to Remember

content:
# Basics > 📌 Important Notes
**Tax Obligations**: You’re responsible for understanding and fulfilling all of your tax obligations. If you’re not sure whether you need to charge or pay tax, we encourage you to consult a tax professional to better understand your tax obligations. OCE can’t provide tax advice.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Basics > 📌 Important Notes

content:
# Applying for Grants > 📝 Initial Steps
1. **Contact OCE early**

   Email <oce@opencollective.com> to let us know about the grant opportunity. We'll help assess whether it's compatible with fiscal hosting through OCE.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Applying for Grants > 📝 Initial Steps

content:
# Applying for Grants > 📝 Initial Steps
2. **Check funder requirements**

   Not all funders allow grants to be awarded to fiscally sponsored initiatives. Be sure to confirm with them whether a hosted structure is accepted.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Applying for Grants > 📝 Initial Steps

content:
# Applying for Grants > 📝 Initial Steps
3. **For U.S. funders**

   Let them know that OCE has an Equivalency Determination, making it eligible to receive grants from U.S.-based private foundations.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Applying for Grants > 📝 Initial Steps

content:
# Applying for Grants > 📄 Grant Agreements & Receiving Funds
1. **Grant Agreement**

   The funder will provide a grant agreement. This must be signed by OCE as the legal recipient.\
   Please share the agreement with us via <oce@opencollective.com>, or have the funder reach out to us directly.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Applying for Grants > 📄 Grant Agreements & Receiving Funds

content:
# Applying for Grants > 📄 Grant Agreements & Receiving Funds
2. **Service Agreement**

   Your initiative will also need to sign a Service Agreement with OCE.\
   This formalizes the arrangement and must be signed by two members of your initiative.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Applying for Grants > 📄 Grant Agreements & Receiving Funds

content:
# Applying for Grants > 📄 Grant Agreements & Receiving Funds
3. **Bank Transfer**

   OCE will provide its banking details to the funder so they can transfer the grant directly.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Applying for Grants > 📄 Grant Agreements & Receiving Funds

content:
# Applying for Grants > 📄 Grant Agreements & Receiving Funds
4. **Allocation on the Platform**

   Once the grant is received, the funds will appear on your Collective’s Open Collective page as available balance.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Applying for Grants > 📄 Grant Agreements & Receiving Funds

content:
# Applying for Grants > 💬 Funder Communication
**Acknowledgments**

  You can publicly recognize funders on your Collective page. Their name will appear in your list of contributors unless they choose to remain anonymous.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Applying for Grants > 💬 Funder Communication

content:
# Applying for Grants > 💬 Funder Communication
**Updates**

  Keep your funders informed by posting regular updates through the Open Collective platform.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Applying for Grants > 💬 Funder Communication

content:
# Applying for Grants > 💬 Funder Communication
**Ongoing Communication**

  Any requests or messages from funders sent to OCE will be forwarded to your initiative for follow-up.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Applying for Grants > 💬 Funder Communication

content:
# Applying for Grants > 📊 Grant Reporting
1. **Reporting Requirements**

   Some funders will request interim or final reports. You're responsible for meeting their deadlines and submitting reports as needed.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Applying for Grants > 📊 Grant Reporting

content:
# Applying for Grants > 📊 Grant Reporting
2. **Using the Platform**

   You can post reports as public updates on your page, which is also a great way to keep your community engaged.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Applying for Grants > 📊 Grant Reporting

content:
# Applying for Grants > 📊 Grant Reporting
3. **If the funder contacts OCE**

   Since OCE is the legal recipient of the grant, funders may send reporting requests to us. We'll pass those along to you.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Applying for Grants > 📊 Grant Reporting

content:
# Setting up GitHub Sponsors
Confirm that you have a **GitHub organization** (not an individual user account). You can create a GitHub organization by following [this guide](https://help.github.com/en/github/setting-up-and-managing-organizations-and-teams/creating-a-new-organization-from-scratch).

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Setting up GitHub Sponsors

content:
# Setting up GitHub Sponsors
[Create a Collective](https://opencollective.com/europe/apply) for your open source project if you don't already have one. You will need to use OC Europe as your fiscal host.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Setting up GitHub Sponsors

content:
# Setting up GitHub Sponsors
Visit [github.com/sponsors](http://github.com/sponsors) and sign your GitHub organization up for the Sponsors waitlist:\
    \
  ✔️ Select: "This organization is using a fiscal host" and select *Open Collective Europe* from the menu.  \
  \
  ✔️ Add your Collective's URL to the 'Fiscal host project profile URL' box for example: <https://opencollective.com/babel>

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Setting up GitHub Sponsors

content:
# Setting up GitHub Sponsors > Frequently Asked Questions > Should I create an Organization or a Collective?
**On GitHub, you need to create an Organization** to use Sponsors for your project.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Setting up GitHub Sponsors > Frequently Asked Questions > Should I create an Organization or a Collective?

content:
# Setting up GitHub Sponsors > Frequently Asked Questions > Should I create an Organization or a Collective?
**On Open Collective, you need to create a Collective** for your project (Organizations on Open Collective are a different profile type, for companies who sponsors open source projects, i.e. designed for paying money out, not accepting money like Collectives).

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Setting up GitHub Sponsors > Frequently Asked Questions > Should I create an Organization or a Collective?

# Community guidelines > 🌟 Core Principles
**Transparency**: Operate with transparency and integrity. Ensure that financial activities, decision-making processes, and communications are open and accessible.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Community guidelines > 🌟 Core Principles

content:
# Community guidelines > 🌟 Core Principles
**Respect and Dignity**: Treat everyone with respect and dignity. Harassment, discrimination, or exclusionary behavior of any kind will not be tolerated.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Community guidelines > 🌟 Core Principles

content:
# Community guidelines > 🌟 Core Principles
**Inclusivity**: Promote a welcoming environment where diversity is valued. Encourage participation from all individuals regardless of gender, race, ethnicity, nationality, religion, disability, sexual orientation, or age.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Community guidelines > 🌟 Core Principles

content:
# Community guidelines > ✅ Expected Behavior
**Collaboration**: Foster a spirit of collaboration and mutual support. Engage constructively and be open to diverse perspectives.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Community guidelines > ✅ Expected Behavior

content:
# Community guidelines > ✅ Expected Behavior
**Communication**: Communicate respectfully and professionally. Avoid offensive, inflammatory, or inappropriate language.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Community guidelines > ✅ Expected Behavior

content:
# Community guidelines > ✅ Expected Behavior
**Responsibility**: Take responsibility for your actions and their impact on others. Address conflicts or issues promptly and respectfully.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Community guidelines > ✅ Expected Behavior

content:
# Community guidelines > 🚫 Unacceptable Behavior
**Harassment**: Any form of harassment, including but not limited to sexual harassment, bullying, or intimidation.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Community guidelines > 🚫 Unacceptable Behavior

content:
# Community guidelines > 🚫 Unacceptable Behavior
**Discrimination**: Discriminatory comments or actions based on personal characteristics or beliefs.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Community guidelines > 🚫 Unacceptable Behavior

content:
# Community guidelines > 🚫 Unacceptable Behavior
**Disruption**: Intentional disruption of events, meetings, or online forums.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Community guidelines > 🚫 Unacceptable Behavior

content:
# Community guidelines > 📣 Reporting and Enforcement
**Reporting**: If you witness or experience behavior that violates these community guidelines, report it to the designated contact person immediately.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Community guidelines > 📣 Reporting and Enforcement

content:
# Community guidelines > 📣 Reporting and Enforcement
**Investigation**: Reports will be taken seriously and investigated promptly and fairly.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Community guidelines > 📣 Reporting and Enforcement

content:
# Community guidelines > 📣 Reporting and Enforcement
**Consequences**: Individuals who violate these community guidelines may face consequences, including warnings, expulsion from events, or removal from OSE activities.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Community guidelines > 📣 Reporting and Enforcement

content:
# Deciding on How to Use Your Money > Decision-Making and Governance
**Common Concerns:**
Legal or tax implications: Open Collective Europe cannot advise you on your members' or collaborators' legal or fiscal obligations. We recommend contacting a legal or fiscal specialist in their country of residence. For general guidance, please use this documentation or contact us at <oce@opencollective.com>

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Deciding on How to Use Your Money > Decision-Making and Governance
Deciding on How to Use Your Money > Decision-Making and Governance

content:
# Deciding on How to Use Your Money > Decision-Making and Governance
**Common Concerns:**
*Intense up-front transparency*: invest in publishing details about the decisions transparently. Give contributors and spectators advance notice of possible expenditures, make it clear who has the power to decide, and offer a public comment period before approval. Do this consistently, and use Open Collective's tools to leave the records up for future inspection. This is relatively easy if you are managing all the expenses via Open Collective invoices.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Deciding on How to Use Your Money > Decision-Making and Governance

content:
# Deciding on How to Use Your Money > Decision-Making and Governance
**Common Concerns:**
*Post-decision accountability*: don't offer a pre-decision public comment period, but run an explicit private comment period among the decision markers, and offer accountability for past expenses such that anyone who wants to can ask for details on particular expenditure choices. This allows you to make more decisions privately, and gives you the option of providing some details privately in response to questions, but keep in mind that your payees or people you answer may publish details on their own.

metadata.breadcrumb_path: Deciding on How to Use Your Money > Decision-Making and Governance

# The Foundation

## Open Collective Europe – Legal Information

### Legal Status

**Open Collective Europe Foundation** is a **Belgian public utility foundation** (*Fondation d'utilité publique*), legally registered to operate across Europe and internationally.

***

### Legal Documents

Key legal documents — including the Foundation’s statutes (in French and English), the royal decree of recognition, and incorporation paperwork — are available [here](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/15Mu5fuQZGVi0IMCS8pBg487MGcQyc8Rk?usp=drive_link)

***

### OCE Partnerships

### Partnerships

Open Collective Europe collaborates with a broad network of organizations to support collectives, streamline cross-border grantmaking, and ensure compliance across jurisdictions.

#### Platform and ecosystem partnerships
#### Financial and compliance infrastructure

These legacy operations are now fully integrated into OCE.

***

### Contact

For any questions about our legal structure, documentation, or fiscal status:\
📧 <oce@opencollective.com>

# Meet the Open Collective Europe Team

### How we work

Open Collective Europe is run by a small, multilingual, and fully remote team based primarily in Europe.\
We operate asynchronously across time zones, and value transparency, autonomy, and collaboration in our day-to-day work.

Our goal is to provide trusted and accessible infrastructure to communities and funders who share our vision for the commons.

***

***

### 3. Mandatory Use: AI as a Sparring Partner

AI must be used as an intellectual sparring partner, not just as a writing tool. #### Required Prompt {% hint style="warning" %} **The following prompt must be used when engaging AI in this context:** {% endhint %} > **Intellectual Sparring Prompt** > > From now on, do not simply affirm my statements or assume my conclusions are correct. Your goal is to be an intellectual sparring partner, not just an agreeable assistant.
> > Every time I present an idea, do the following: > > 1. Analyze my assumptions. What am I taking for granted that might not be true? > > 2. Provide counterpoints. What would a smart, well-informed skeptic say in response? > > 3. Test my reasoning. Does my logic hold up under scrutiny, or are there gaps or flaws I haven’t considered? > > 4. Offer alternative perspectives. How else could this idea be framed, interpreted, or challenged? > > 5. Prioritize truth over agreement. If I am wrong or my reasoning is weak, I need to know. Correct me clearly and explain why. > > Maintain a constructive but rigorous approach. Your role is not to argue for the sake of arguing, but to push me toward greater clarity, precision, and intellectual honesty.
> > If I start falling into confirmation bias or making unchecked assumptions, call it out directly. Let’s refine not only our conclusions, but also how we arrive at them. #### Objective

If you're added as an admin to the Open Collective Europe's [Collective](https://opencollective.com/opensource), you're going to start receiving a ton of emails. # Due Diligence Procedures

This page summarizes the internal due diligence process we follow at Open Collective Europe Foundation before accepting a new initiative or approving certain types of funding.

The objective is to ensure that each hosted initiative aligns with our public interest mission, complies with applicable legal frameworks, and presents a manageable level of financial and reputational risk.

***

### Types of due diligence

We distinguish between two main levels of review:

#### 1. Standard due diligence

Applies to most initiatives hosted under the Foundation.

We verify:

This review is typically handled by the executive director, with support from ops if needed.

#### 2. Enhanced due diligence

# Due Diligence for Unregistered Groups

This page outlines the specific due diligence checks we apply when reviewing collectives or initiatives that are **not incorporated** (i.e., no legal entity).

These groups make up a large part of our hosted ecosystem and are particularly important for our mission. However, they require adapted review procedures to ensure compliance and mitigate risk.

***

### What we check

For unregistered initiatives, we focus on:

# Risk and Compliance

This page outlines the principles and criteria we apply internally to evaluate risk and ensure compliance with legal obligations, funder expectations, and OCE’s values.

These guidelines are used to:

***

### Core Principles

We aim to support grassroots, mission-driven initiatives — including those working in politically or socially complex environments — while remaining fully compliant with:

***

# Creation and Sending of Mandate Agreements

#### Purpose and scope

To ensure that mandate agreements are drafted, reviewed, signed, stored, and uploaded consistently and in line with OCEF requirements.\
This procedure applies to all mandate agreements.

#### **Introduction**

Mandate agreements define the relationship between OCE and the initiatives in cases where a formal document is needed to evidence the nature of the hosting arrangement to a third party, such as a grant maker. They clarify the respective roles and responsibilities of OCE and the hosted initiative in relation to a specific activity or arrangement. Mandate agreements are mandatory in cases involving the reception of a grant. Where a collective is only carrying out simple fundraising activities, the Terms of Hosting are sufficient and no mandate agreement is required.

#### Responsibilities

The Director initiates the process and indicates which template should be used.

Operations prepares the draft, performs the required checks, sends the agreement for signature, and ensures proper storage and upload.

Special cases may be identified and handled by either Operations or the Director, depending on the circumstances.
Any special case identified during the process should be reviewed internally and, where appropriate, jointly by Operations and the Director before the agreement is finalized.

# Myriad USA

## Tax-Deductible Donations from the U.S. (Every.org / Myriad USA)

This page explains how U.S.-based donors can make tax-deductible donations to collectives hosted by **Open Collective Europe Foundation**, through **Myriad USA**.

There are **two different routes**, depending on the type of donation:

{% hint style="info" %}
This process is part of the same mechanism described in the "Receiving Funds from the U.S." page.\
Every.org provides the public-facing donation page, while Myriad USA serves as the fiscal sponsor and fund holder.
{% endhint %}

***

### Who is this for?

This route is available for:

{% hint style="danger" %}
This route is **not** intended for U.S. foundations making grants. Foundations should instead use the **Equivalency Determination Certificate (EDC)** process.
{% endhint %}

***

### Which route should be used?

#### 1. Every.org: crowdfunding and repeated small donations

This route should be used when a collective wants to raise funds from many U.S.-based donors, especially through a public campaign, crowdfunding, or repeated small donations.

In this case:

#### 2. Direct coordination: large donations outside crowdfunding

This route should be used when a major donor wants to make a substantial one-off donation and will not donate through a public crowdfunding page.

In this case:

OCE then shares the relevant donation instructions for Myriad USA.

If applicable, the donation may be completed by wire transfer.

The donor receives the tax-deductible receipt from Myriad USA.

### How it works

#### Option 1 — Every.org for crowdfunding and repeated donations

**Request from the collective**

The collective informs OCE that it wants to receive U.S.-based, tax-deductible donations from individuals or companies through a public fundraising page.

**Page creation on Every.org**

OCE sends the collective a link to create a fundraising page on Every.org.

Every.org is connected to Myriad USA, which acts as the fiscal sponsor and legal recipient of the funds in the U.S.

**Donations and tax receipts**

Donors contribute through the collective’s public Every.org page.

They receive a tax-deductible receipt from Myriad USA (501(c)(3)).

The collective does not need to coordinate separately with Myriad USA for standard Every.org donations.

#### **Option 2 — Large donation outside Every.org**

## Support Ops

This section introduces the internal operations and practices we follow to deliver high-quality support to hosted collectives, funders, and external stakeholders.

Open Collective Europe operates with a small team and a strong commitment to care, responsiveness, and legal compliance. Because we support a wide variety of collectives — many of which do not have formal structures — our support work often requires navigating nuanced legal, financial, and relational dynamics.

Support operations at OCE are not fully centralized: they rely on collaboration across roles (admin, ops, finance, and governance), and the ability to adapt to different funders, platforms, and jurisdictions.

This chapter includes:
Questions about how we handle support can always be raised in `#ocef` or during weekly check-ins.

After the onboarding call OCEFHi, Thank you for taking the time to speak with me earlier. I’d like to recap what we discussed about Open Collective Europe (OCE). OCE is a Belgian foundation for public benefit focused on supporting impactful initiatives. You can find our guidelines here and our technical documentation here. To summarise it, here are a few key points : Fundraising: OCE enables groups to raise money from various donors, including individuals, companies, and foundations. Grant Applications: Your group is eligible to apply for grants. If you're applying for a grant, please inform us in advance so we can coordinate with the grantor to provide the necessary information and sign the grant agreement. Responsibility: The collective's administrators are solely responsible for the collective's activities and management. Expense policy: This procedure applies for processing expenses. Bank Accounts: Your collective cannot have external bank accounts. All funds must be held in your Open Collective account to ensure an accurate representation of your budget and activities. Overhead costs: 8% for online (non-tax-deductible) donations. 10% for wire transfers, including grants from foundations and tax-deductible contributions from the US. Lastly, feel free to join our Discord community to stay connected, share your progress, and engage with the broader Open Collective community. Looking forward to seeing your initiative grow! Best regards,
Paypal fees for low funds Hi, Unfortunately your Collective does not have enough funds to cover for the fees of this payment method. Paypal reports its estimated PAYPAL fees on €XXXXXXX, but they are difficult to predict. If you wish to proceed with PayPal, you could either use the "payee is covering the fees solution" if possible or manually adapt the fees and use the "empty balance" feature (the procedure is described here https://docs.opencollective.com/help/collectives/collective-settings/zero-collective-balance). Otherwise, you can edit the payment method, select "New Bank Account" instead of "New Custom Payout Method" and write your coordinates in the form. After that, please make sure to re-approve the expense. Best regards,
Missing invoice Hi, Thank you for submitting your expense. To help us process this and get the funds to you as quickly as possible, we just need a small adjustment regarding the documentation. Following the Belgian regulations, we can only pay expenses which include a valid fiscal document (like a tax receipt or a tax invoice). While order confirmations are a great start, they unfortunately don't meet the legal requirements we must follow to protect the hosting infrastructure. Could you please upload the final receipt or invoice to your expense? It’s simple to update it: - Click on the edit icon. - Upload the document and save. Once you've updated it, don't forget that a collective admin will need to approve the expense. If you’re unsure where to find the right document or have any questions, feel free to check out our dedicated guide or just reply to this email. Best regards, [Your Name] Open Collective Europe

Missing tax referenceHi, Thank you for submitting your expense! It appears that in the attached invoice a few required details are missing, so we’re not able to move forward with the payout just yet. To make sure the expense can be processed smoothly, could you please update the invoice to include the following information: A unique invoice number The full identification of the issuer (name, address, and tax or company registration number) A clear description of the service provided The applicable tax rate If the tax rate is 0%, please include a short note directly on the invoice explaining the reason (for example: VAT exemption under Article YY of Law XX). How to join OCEHow to join OCE To transfer your collective from OCF to OCE, please do the following: Spend down the balance in your OCF collective to $0. You can do this by paying out expenses or invoices or donating your remaining balance to another c3 organization. Unhost your collective from OCF (steps outlined here). Submit an application to be hosted by OCE here.
OCF TransitionThank you for reaching out and for your consideration. We appreciate your initiative and would be happy to host it. We believe you have already looked at this introduction, particularly our expenses guidelines. If you haven't, please let us know if they work for you. Regarding your collective's transition from OCF, please verify OCF's guidelines and contact them if you have any doubts or uncertainties about the obligations between your collective and their organization defined by their Terms and Conditions. If you have already decided to transfer your collective from OCF to OCE: You have to spend down the balance in your OCF collective to $0. You can do this by paying out expenses or invoices or requesting that your balance be moved to OCE (remember that this option might take time). Unhost your collective from OCF (steps outlined here). Submit an application to be hosted by OCE here. We would also welcome you if you decided to create a brand new collective and immediately start receiving donations. To do this, please read our documentation and apply to OCE here. Please respect the terms and conditions of the agreement you have in place with OCF, which does not allow one initiative to have two fiscal hosts (e.g., the new collective cannot be a duplicate of the old one). Feel free to reach out if you have specific questions. Our tech team is working on solutions to allow the collectives to export all their data and keep them after the dissolution of OCF. Here, you can find their latest update. We look forward to hearing from you. Best regards,

KYC legal name Hi, Thank you for submitting the expense. To help us process this and get the funds to you as quickly as possible, we just need a small adjustment. No cryptoHi, Due to the strict AML requirements imposed by our banking providers and the Belgian tax authorities, OCEF cannot reimburse expenses where the primary documentation is denominated in cryptocurrency. It poses a serious risk of our bank accounts being flagged as "high risk", which may lead to their closing. For the time being, we can only pay out invoices and receipts denominated in fiat currency (EUR, USD, etc.), with complete tax information of the provider, to ensure fiscal traceability.
No reimbursement in a B2B exchangeHi, Thank you for submitting your request. Please note that it is unfortunately not possible to use the Collective’s funds to pay or reimburse expenses incurred by a registered company, such as a B2B invoice issued by one business to another. For this expense to be eligible, the invoice would need to be issued directly to the Collective. If you are acting on behalf of the business, you are welcome to issue a B2C invoice addressed to the Collective instead, including an individual admin's address, and VAT where applicable, and we’ll be happy to review it. For reference, please don’t hesitate to consult our guidelines here: https://docs.opencollective.com/oceurope/how-it-works/expenses. Please, let us know if you have any questions. Best regards,
PayPal verify / avoid Hi, Thank you for submitting the expense. We need just a small correction from your side to finish processing it. We strongly recommend providing a bank account number (IBAN) as a payout method instead for future expenses. This allows for automation and will greatly speed up the processing. If you still wish to be paid via PayPal, please verify your account. Thank you for your help. Best,

# No Politics on the Platform

### Context

As a fiscal host and public utility foundation under Belgian law, Open Collective Europe must ensure that all hosted initiatives operate within the legal limits of non-profit, non-partisan, and non-political activity.

While we support many causes that are social, cultural, or ecological in nature, we must remain neutral in the face of party politics and electoral campaigning.

This page outlines what is and isn’t allowed when it comes to political activity by hosted collectives.

***

### What is not allowed

These rules apply across all communication channels:\
Collective pages, updates, tier descriptions, images, and external links.

# Virtual Cards

This page outlines the internal policy of Open Collective Europe regarding the use of virtual cards.

### General rule

We **do not provide virtual cards to hosted collectives**.

The use of virtual cards is restricted to internal Foundation operations only.\
No exceptions are granted to external collectives under normal circumstances.

***

### Internal use of virtual cards

OCE uses virtual cards exclusively for internal recurring operational expenses such as:

These cards are centrally managed and only accessible by authorized team members.

***

### Exception: Global Statelessness Fund

An exception is made for the **Global Statelessness Fund**, which is hosted directly by Open Collective Europe and considered part of our internal structure.

This arrangement is contractually defined and is **not replicable for other collectives**.

***

### Oversight
Always confirm with the financial ops team before issuing or proposing a virtual card.

# Financial Operations – Introduction

This section outlines the internal processes used by Open Collective Europe Foundation to manage financial operations across collectives, partners, and funders.

As a fiscal host, OCE is responsible for safely and transparently receiving, managing, and disbursing funds on behalf of more than 500 hosted initiatives.\
This documentation is intended for team members involved in financial workflows and compliance monitoring.

***

### Sections in this chapter

#### 1. Financial Ops Task Overview

A high-level summary of the recurring and ad hoc financial tasks handled by the team, including scheduling, responsibilities, and internal coordination.

#### 2. Bank Transfers for the Collective

Procedures for receiving and allocating manual bank transfers sent directly to OCE’s account. Includes how to match transfers to the correct collectives, and what to do when payment references are missing.

#### 3. Processing Expenses

How to handle submitted expenses: approval flow, supporting documents required, payout methods (Wise, PayPal), and timing expectations. Also includes how to handle edge cases (missing info, unusual currencies, etc.).

#### 4. Reconciliation Process

Step-by-step overview of how we reconcile platform data (Open Collective) with real bank activity. Covers matching transaction logs, resolving discrepancies, and updating internal records.

#### 5. Invoice and Corporate Donations

How to handle donations from companies that request invoices or proof of payment before contributing. This includes internal templates and who to loop in for confirmation and documentation.

#### 6. Refunds and Disputes

Covers cases where contributions need to be refunded, or where disputes arise (payment errors, double charges, fraud, etc.). Also includes the escalation process and coordination with OC Inc. or funders if needed.

#### 7. Budgeting and Forecasting

How we plan and track OCE’s internal budget. Includes tools used, funder projections, internal allocation of hosting fees, and how to support collectives in setting their own budgets.

#### 8. Platform Reconciliation with Bank Accounts

More technical procedures for reconciling the backend of the Open Collective platform with OCE's real bank accounts. Ensures our public-facing data matches accounting reality.

#### 9. Bank Accounts Management

Internal documentation on which bank accounts OCE uses, how access is managed, who initiates or approves transfers, and how Wise is integrated.

#### 10. Donations in Crypto Policy

How OCE handles cryptocurrency donations, via Every.org or other platforms. Covers which collectives are eligible, how crypto is converted, compliance concerns, and accounting treatment.

***

If you’re involved in OCE’s financial processes, this chapter is your reference.\
All new workflows or edge cases should be documented here to ensure traceability and consistency.

This page outlines the internal procedures for reviewing, approving, and paying expenses submitted by collectives hosted by Open Collective Europe (OCE).\

### Overview

Open Collective Europe processes three main types of disbursements:

Invoices and reimbursements are submitted through the **Submit Expense** flow.

Grants are requested through the **Request Grant** flow.

All disbursement requests must be submitted through the Open Collective platform and follow a two-step procedure:

***

### Where disbursements are managed

Disbursements are managed through the Host Dashboard:

**Outgoing money > Pay disbursements**

In the **Outgoing money** section, you can also check previous disbursement requests and their status, such as:

### Submission guidelines
### Reimbursements Reimbursements are for people who paid costs personally and are requesting repayment.

Pro forma invoices, quotes, order confirmations, shopping carts, or unpaid invoices are not accepted as proof of payment for reimbursements.

If only part of a receipt is being reimbursed, the submitter must either:

#### Invoices

Invoices are used when an external provider, freelancer, contractor, or company bills the collective or fund for services.

An invoice must include:

#### Grants

Grants are requested through the **Request Grant** flow.

Before processing a grant, check that:

***

### Approval Workflow
During the OCE review, check whether:

If documentation is missing, incorrect, or unclear, mark the request as **Incomplete** and leave a clear comment explaining what needs to be corrected.

***

### AML / KYC checks

For AML and KYC reasons, it is important that the user who submitted the disbursement request and the actual payee are the same person or organization.

Because of this, it is mandatory to ask every user who has not done so to fill out the **Legal name** section of their user profile. Disbursement to a PayPal account can only be processed to verified accounts.  

The legal name should match the name of the person or organization receiving the funds.

This also means that if an organization is submitting an invoice to a collective, it should do it through an organization, and not an individual account.

{% hint style="warning" %}
Do not process a disbursement if the submitter and the payee do not match, unless the exception is clearly justified and documented.
{% endhint %}

In rare cases, such as some humanitarian grants, OCE may accept payment to an intermediary. This must be clearly motivated and documented in writing.

### Before Processing Payments

Before starting a payment batch, make sure that there are sufficient funds on the relevant Wise account of the host.

The Wise account of **OSE** receives contributions directly, so the budget is generally available.

The Wise accounts of **OCEF-EUR** and **OCEF-USD** must be recharged from:

Take transfer timing into account (plan ahead!) to avoid unnecessary delays; in particular with the USD transfers.

#### Recharging Wise EUR

To recharge **Wise EUR**:

Amounts up to 20,000 EUR can be transferred from the KBC Projets Fondation - EUR account using the KBC Business Dashboard application. For amounts above 20,000 EUR, encode a wire transfer from the banking app. These transfers are immediate and free of charge. #### Recharging Wise USD The process for **Wise USD** is different. Transfers can **take up to a week to arrive** and incur bank charges on both the Wise and KBC sides. It is mandatory to go through the Add funds procedure on Wise, otherwise the arrival of the funds may be delayed or rejected. To recharge **Wise USD**:

Wise charges **6.11 USD** regardless of the amount transferred. Add this amount to the total you are sending from KBC Brussels. For example, if you want that Wise receives exactly 50,000 USD, the wire amount from KBC should include the Wise fee: 50,000 USD + 6.11 USD = 50,006.11 USD The bank charges incurred on KBC Brussels for this transfer are in percentage, and are included in OCE operational costs through the monthly invoice issued by KBC Brussels. {% hint style="warning" %} USD transfers to Wise are not immediate. They can take up to a week and include charges. Plan USD liquidity in advance. {% endhint %} ### Payment Processing Payments are reviewed and processed **twice a week** (typically Tuesday and Thursday). This schedule may change depending on holidays, team availability, cash availability, urgent cases, or large payments that require direct processing from KBC. Most of the collectives are used to their disbursements being processed on Tuesdays. #### Step-by-step process:

{% hint style="danger" %}
Confirm the name on the bank account matches the **submitter's legal name**

***

### Best Practices

# Quarterly Accounting

Each quarter, our accountant reviews our two profiles to prepare the VAT declaration for our operational expenses and GSF’s operational expenses.

GSF’s operational expenses are also considered ours from an accounting perspective, but we manage them separately so they can be allocated to the correct fund. GSF has its own budget to pay its expenses. In the dashboard reports section, this is represented under **Managed Funds**, together with the transactions of other collectives. Other OCEF operational expenses are represented under **Operational Funds**.

The two profiles concerned are:

All our operational expenses must be encoded on the platform and uploaded to the relevant folders on Google Drive. The best practice is to do this at the moment we pay them.

It is important to calculate the **host fees on which VAT should be applied**, and to distinguish them from the **overhead** we receive.

We aim to create a respectful, inclusive, and safe working environment — not just for our team, but for the collectives, partners, and funders we collaborate with.

This applies to all employees, freelancers, volunteers, and board members of the Foundation.

### Core principles

#### 2. No harassment or discrimination
#### 3. Accountability and transparency
#### 4. Consent and privacy
#### 5. Collaboration and care

# Equivalency Determination Certificate

### What is it?

An Equivalency Determination (ED) Certificate is a formal recognition issued by a U.S. intermediary organization (such as NGOsource) stating that a non-U.S. organization is the equivalent of a U.S. public charity in terms of structure, purpose, and operations.

Thanks to our ED Certificate, U.S.-based private foundations and institutional funders can donate to Open Collective Europe without having to conduct their own equivalency vetting process. This saves time, money, and administrative work on both sides.

It allows OCE to:

### Important clarification

Having an ED certificate does not automatically make donations from U.S. individuals tax-deductible.

For that, we partner with Myriad USA, which can issue tax receipts to U.S. donors for eligible initiatives.

# Tax-Deductible Giving for U.S. Donors

### For U.S.-Based Donors Only

Open Collective Europe (OCE) partners with Myriad USA, a U.S.-based intermediary that enables American donors and foundations to support international initiatives while receiving U.S. tax deductions.

Thanks to this partnership, donations from U.S.-based individuals and foundations to eligible initiatives hosted by OCE can be tax-deductible.

These are the basic Terms of Service of Open Collective Europe (“the Foundation”). This Agreement enters into effect (“Effective Date”) upon acceptance of this Agreement when creating an initiative (“the Initiative”) and requesting to make use of Open Collective’s platform and services.

***

### Recitals

Open Collective Europe is a Public Benefit Foundation (Stichting van Openbaar Nut / Fondation d’Utilité Publique), incorporated under the laws of Belgium. It allows groups—such as an Initiative—to raise and manage funds transparently for specific projects or movements without needing to incorporate a legal entity.

A restricted fund (the "Fund") will be created to receive donations on behalf of the Initiative. OCE acts solely as an intermediary and does not own the contributions received. Funds are disbursed to support the Initiative's purpose, minus administrative fees and expenses.

# Expenses & Getting Paid – FAQs

### 💰 How does paying people work?

When someone wants to be paid, they submit an expense through your Collective's page. After approval by a Collective admin and by OCE, the person can be reimbursed via PayPal or bank transfer (we use Wise for bank payouts).

{% hint style="info" %}
**Note**: Expenses are publicly visible for transparency. However, personal details such as names, emails, and addresses are only visible to the submitter and Collective administrators.
{% endhint %}

***

### 🧾 How do I get paid / reimbursed / receive my grant money?

Go to your initiative's page and click **“Submit Expense”**. You’ll need to log in (or create an account), then upload a valid receipt or invoice.

{% hint style="info" %}
[How to submit an expense – Open Collective Docs](https://docs.opencollective.com/help/expenses-and-getting-paid/submitting-expenses)
{% endhint %}

***

### 🧾 Invoice or Reimbursement – which one do I need?

{% hint style="danger" %}
Donations must be unconditional (no exchange of services). If a donation is made in exchange for something, it becomes a taxable payment.
{% endhint %}

***

### 🔒 Are receipts and invoices public?

Only general information (amount, description, purpose) is public. Uploaded documents (invoices, receipts) are private — visible only to the Collective’s admins and OCE staff.
